title: Nephrodium chrysocoma
intro:
There is limited information on Nephrodium chrysocoma. However, I can provide information on Nephrolepis chrysocoma.

Nephrolepis chrysocoma, also known as the Boston fern or silver vairagum fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Nephrolepidaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Nephrolepis chrysocoma include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Nephrodium chrysocoma, also known as the London brake, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. The classification of Nephrodium chrysocoma is as follows: it belongs to the genus Nephrodium, which is now considered to be a synonym of Dryopteris, but some sources still use the name Nephrodium for this species. As a member of the Dryopteridaceae family, Nephrodium chrysocoma is a type of wood fern. The classification can be broken down further into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. Within the Polypodiales order, Nephrodium chrysocoma is specifically classified as Dryopteris chrysocoma within the family Dryopteridaceae.
